title: Network security dynamic access control and policy enforcement
abstract: A network security system and method implements dynamic access control for a protected resource using run-time contextual information. In some embodiments, the network security system and method implements a dynamic access ticket scheme for access control where the access ticket is based on run-time application context. In other embodiments, the network security system and method implements policy enforcement actions in response to detected violations using application programming interface (API) to effectively block detected policy violations without negatively impacting the operation of the application or the user of the application. In some embodiments, the network security system uses enterprise social collaboration tools to interact with the end-user or with the system administrator in the event of detected security incidents.
claims:
Claims 1. A computer-implemented method for providing access control to a protected resource using run-time transaction data between a client application and a service application in a zero trust environment, the service application accessing the protected resource in response to a request from the client application, the computer-implemented method comprising: deploying a ticket-based access control layer providing an access control function to the protected resource; monitoring the run-time transaction information between the client application and the service application, the client application communicating with the service application to request data from the protected resource; receiving, at an access control manager, the run-time transaction information; generating, at the access control manager, a validated access ticket using at least in part the run-time transaction information; providing the validated access ticket to the ticket-based access control layer; and applying, at the ticket-based access control layer, access control rules using the validated access ticket to grant or deny access to the protected resource in response to a request for data from the service application. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: at the ticket-based access control layer, denying access to the protected resource in response to an access ticket not being provided by the service application to the ticket-based access control layer to be validated by the validated access ticket. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: receiving, at the ticket-based access control layer, a service access ticket from the service application; at the ticket-based access control layer, granting access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket being validated using the validated access ticket; and at the ticket-based access control layer, denying access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket not being validated using the validated access ticket. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: 

 selecting, at the access control manager, a ticket generation method; providing the selected ticket generation method to the service application; generating, at the access control manager, the validated access ticket using the ticket generation method and using at least in part the run-time transaction information; receiving, at the ticket-based access control layer, a service access ticket generated at the service application using the selected ticket generating method; applying, at the ticket-based access control layer, the access control rules to validate the service access ticket using the validated access ticket; at the ticket-based access control layer, granting access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket being validated using the validated access ticket; and at the ticket-based access control layer, denying access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket not being validated using the validated access ticket. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 4, wherein electing the ticket generation method comprises: electing, at the access control manager, a method to generate the access ticket using at least in part the run-time transaction information. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: receiving, at the access control manager, a request from the client application requesting a client access ticket; generating, at the access control manager, the client access ticket and providing the client access ticket to the client application; monitoring the run-time transaction information for the client access ticket transmitted from the client application to the service application; receiving, at the access control manager, the run-time transaction information comprising the client access ticket; generating, at the access control manager, the validated access ticket comprising validating the client access ticket and providing the client access ticket as the validated access ticket; receiving, at the ticket-based access control layer, a service access ticket from the service application; 

 applying, at the ticket-based access control layer, the access control rules to validate the service access ticket using the validated access ticket; at the ticket-based access control layer, granting access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket being validated using the validated access ticket; and at the ticket-based access control layer, denying access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket not being validated using the validated access ticket. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 6, wherein the service access ticket comprises the client access ticket transmitted to the service application from the client application, the service application forwarding the client access ticket to the ticket-based access control layer as the service access ticket. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: receiving, at the access control manager, a request from the service application requesting a service access ticket and including a first session token; receiving, at the access control manager, the run-time transaction information including a second session token identifying the connection session between the client application and the service application; validating, at the access control manager, the first session token using the second session token; in response to the first session token being validated, generating, at the access control manager, the service access ticket and providing the service access ticket to the service application; generating, at the access control manager, the validated access ticket comprising providing the service access ticket as the validated access ticket; receiving, at the ticket-based access control layer, the service access ticket from the service application; applying, at the ticket-based access control layer, the access control rules to validate the service access ticket using the validated access ticket; at the ticket-based access control layer, granting access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket being validated using the validated access ticket; and at the ticket-based access control layer, denying access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket not being validated using the validated access ticket. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: deploying a ticket injection module in a communication path between the client application and the service application; at the ticket injection module, injecting an access ticket in the run-time transaction between the client application and the service application using the run-time transaction information; receiving, at the access control manager, the run-time transaction information from the monitoring comprises receiving the injected access ticket in the monitored run-time transaction information; generating, at the access control manager, the validated access ticket comprising validating the injected access ticket using run-time transaction information and providing the injected access ticket as the validated access ticket; receiving, at the ticket-based access control layer, a service access ticket from the service application; applying, at the ticket-based access control layer, the access control rules to validate the service access ticket using the validated access ticket; at the ticket-based access control layer, granting access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket being validated using the validated access ticket; and at the ticket-based access control layer, denying access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket not being validated using the validated access ticket. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 9, wherein the service access ticket comprises the injected access ticket transmitted to the service application on the communication path, the service application forwarding the injected access ticket to the ticket-based access control layer as the service access ticket. 11. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: deploying a first ticket injection module in a first communication path between the client application and the service application; at the first ticket injection module, injecting an access ticket in the run-time transaction between the client application and the service application using the run-time transaction information; 

 receiving, at the access control manager, the run-time transaction information from the monitoring comprises receiving the injected access ticket in the monitored run-time transaction information; generating, at the access control manager, the validated access ticket comprising validating the injected access ticket using run-time transaction information and providing the injected access ticket as the validated access ticket; deploying a second ticket injection module in a second communication path between the service application and the ticket-based access control layer; at the second ticket injection module, injecting a service access ticket provided by the access control manager in the run-time transaction between the service application and the ticket-based access control layer; receiving, at the ticket-based access control layer, the service access ticket from the service application; applying, at the ticket-based access control layer, the access control rules to validate the service access ticket using the validated access ticket; at the ticket-based access control layer, granting access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket being validated using the validated access ticket; and at the ticket-based access control layer, denying access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket not being validated using the validated access ticket. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 9, wherein the service access ticket comprises the validated access ticket which is related to the injected access ticket generated by the first ticket injection module. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: in response to the ticket-based access control layer denying access to the protected resource by the service application, generating, at a policy enforcement manager, an application programming interface request to instruct the service application to logout of the connection session. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: in response to the ticket-based access control layer denying access to the protected resource by the service application, generating, at a policy enforcement manager, an 

 application programming interface request to instruct the service application to restore deleted resources or quarantine new resources stored on the protected resource. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: 5 in response to the ticket-based access control layer denying access to the protected resource by the service application, generating, at a policy enforcement manager, a notification event on a social collaboration tool or a messaging tool to notify the client application of the denial of access. io 16. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: providing an inline security module in the communication path between the client application and the service application; in response to the ticket-based access control layer denying access to the protected resource by the service application, identifying, at a policy enforcement manager, the is connection session associated with the access denial; providing the identified connection session to the inline security module; detecting at the inline security module run-time transaction associated with the identified connection session; redirecting the run-time transaction to a deception honeypot module, the deception 20 honeypot module providing decoyed run-time transaction to the identified connection session. 17. A security system for providing access control to a protected resource using run-time transaction data between a client application and a service application in a zero trust 25 environment, the service application accessing the protected resource in response to a request from the client application, the system comprising: 

 a ticket-based access control layer providing an access control function to the protected resource; a transaction monitor configured to monitor run-time transaction data between a client application and a service application, the client application communicating with the service application to request data from the protected resource; and an access control manager configured to receive the run-time transaction information, to generate a validated access ticket using at least in part the run-time transaction information, to provide the validated access ticket to the ticket-based access control layer, wherein the ticket-based access control layer applies access control rules using the validated access ticket to grant or deny access to the protected resource in response to a request for data from the service application. 18. The security system of claim 17, wherein the ticket-based access control layer denies access to the protected resource in response to an access ticket not being provided by the service application to the ticket-based access control layer to be validated by the validated access ticket 19. The security system of claim 17, wherein the ticket-based access control layer receives a service access ticket from the service application, the ticket-based access control layer grants access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket being validated using the validated access ticket and denies access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket not being validated using the validated access ticket. 20. The security system of claim 17, further comprising: a ticket injection module deployed in a communication path between the client application and the service application, the ticket injection module being configured to inject an access ticket in the run-time transaction between the client application and the service application using the run-time transaction information, wherein the access control manager is configured to receive the injected access ticket in the monitored run-time transaction information, to validate the injected access ticket using run-time transaction information, and to provide the injected access ticket as the validated access ticket; and 

 wherein the ticket-based access control layer receives a service access ticket from the service application, the ticket-based access control layer applying the access control rules to validate the service access ticket using the validated access ticket, the ticket-based access control layer granting access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket being validated using the validated access ticket and denying access to the protected resource in response to the service access ticket not being validated using the validated access ticket.
